themes_heading: US-Iran war reignites · Kimi K3 second DeepSeek shock · Meta-Anthropic $10B · Fedorov fired
synthesis_md:
The [June 17 Versailles ceasefire](https://www.cbsnews.com/live-updates/us-iran-war-trump-ceasefire-talks-strait-of-hormuz/) between the US and Iran has collapsed. After Iran struck Strait of Hormuz targets, Trump declared the MOU over, and US forces have since run [seven consecutive nights of airstrikes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_war) inside Iran — hitting logistics, weapons storage, and maritime capabilities across Tehran and the south. Oil is back above eighty dollars a barrel. With Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh already signaling hawkishness, the inflation picture heading into fall just got harder.

In Ukraine, Zelenskyy [fired defense minister Mykhailo Fedorov](https://www.npr.org/2026/07/16/g-s1-133920/ukraine-defense-minister-fired) — the architect of the drone warfare strategy — citing an irreconcilable conflict with military command. His likely replacement is a traditional police general. More than a thousand people gathered on Kyiv's Maidan chanting for Fedorov's return, a rare eruption of public disagreement with Zelenskyy.

Two things arrived together in AI on Friday. Moonshot AI unveiled [Kimi K3](https://venturebeat.com/technology/chinas-moonshot-ai-releases-kimi-k3-the-largest-open-source-model-ever-rivaling-top-u-s-systems) — a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight model that benchmarks near the top US frontier systems. Markets declared it a [second DeepSeek moment](https://fortune.com/2026/07/17/tech-stocks-global-selloff-as-investors-ai-semiconductor-chips/): the semiconductor index down 20%+ from June, TSMC fell 7%, Nvidia briefly lost the most-valuable-company title to Apple. Simultaneously, [Meta and Anthropic confirmed talks](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/17/anthropic-meta-ai-compute.html) over a $10 billion, two-year compute lease — Meta monetizing spare GPU capacity rather than ceding revenue to CoreWeave.

## What this means for you

Kimi K3 weights drop July 27 — put it in your eval stack alongside Mistral. If it holds up in real-world tasks, the cost of frontier intelligence keeps falling and model-agnostic architecture keeps making more sense. The Meta-Anthropic deal signals Anthropic is diversifying its GPU supply chain; if Meta enters cloud compute seriously, API pricing and reliability should improve.

The Nordic split is worth noting: [Stargate Norway](https://openai.com/index/introducing-stargate-norway/) is targeting 100,000 NVIDIA GPUs in Narvik by year-end on cheap hydropower. In the same week, the government [banned generative AI in grades 1–7](https://all-things-nordic.com/2026/06/22/norway-draws-a-line-on-ai-in-schools/) starting August. Infrastructure at national scale, restriction at the classroom level — a useful frame for the European AI moment more broadly.

Iran and oil are the macro wildcard: above $80, the Fed's room narrows and Q3 rate expectations shift.
english_script:
Good morning. Here's your briefing for Sunday, July nineteenth.

The ceasefire between the United States and Iran, signed at Versailles last month, has effectively collapsed. American forces have now struck targets inside Iran for seven consecutive nights — hitting logistics infrastructure, weapons storage sites, and maritime capabilities across Tehran and the south. Oil is back above eighty dollars a barrel. With Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh already signaling hawkish intentions, the inflation and interest rate picture heading into fall just got significantly murkier.

In Ukraine, President Zelenskyy fired his defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov — the tech-forward official who built Ukraine's drone warfare program. The stated reason was an irreconcilable conflict with the military command. His expected replacement is a traditional police general, which explains why more than a thousand protesters gathered in Kyiv's central square chanting for Fedorov's return — a rare and striking sign of public disagreement with Zelenskyy.

Two things happened in AI on Friday that are best understood together. China's Moonshot AI unveiled Kimi K3 — a model with two point eight trillion parameters, making it the largest open-weight AI system in the world. On benchmarks, it rivals the best American frontier models. Markets immediately called it a second DeepSeek moment: the semiconductor index is now down more than twenty percent from its June peak, Taiwan Semiconductor fell seven percent despite reporting a seventy-seven percent rise in quarterly profit, and Nvidia briefly lost the title of the world's most valuable company to Apple. The full model weights are due for public release on July twenty-seventh.

On the same day, Meta and Anthropic confirmed they are in early talks over a ten-billion-dollar compute lease — with Meta offering spare GPU capacity from its AI infrastructure over a two-year period.

From Norway: the government has banned generative AI in elementary schools starting this fall, covering grades one through seven. Prime Minister Støre cited concerns about children's reading and writing development. This lands in the same week that Stargate Norway confirmed it is on track to deliver one hundred thousand NVIDIA GPUs to a data center in Narvik by year-end, powered by cheap hydropower.

And this afternoon, Spain and Argentina play the FIFA World Cup final.

That's your Sunday briefing.
norwegian_script:
God morgen. Her er din oppdatering for søndag den nittende juli.

Våpenhvilen mellom USA og Iran, undertegnet i Versailles forrige måned, er i praksis kollapset. Amerikanske styrker har nå angrepet mål inne i Iran syv netter på rad — mot logistikkinfrastruktur, våpenlagre og maritime installasjoner i Teheran og sør i landet. Oljeprisen er tilbake over åtti dollar fatet. Med Federal Reserve-sjef Kevin Warsh, som allerede signaliserer en restriktiv pengepolitikk, er inflasjons- og rentebildet blitt betraktelig mer uoversiktlig inn mot høsten.

I Ukraina sparket president Zelenskyj forsvarsminister Mykhailo Fedorov — mannen som bygde Ukrainas droneprogram. Den oppgitte grunnen var en uforsonlig konflikt med militærledelsen. Den forventede etterfølgeren er en tradisjonell politigeneral, noe som forklarer hvorfor over tusen demonstranter samlet seg på Kyjivs Majdan og ropte på at Fedorov skulle komme tilbake — et sjeldent offentlig tegn på misnøye med Zelenskyjs beslutninger.

To ting skjedde innen kunstig intelligens fredag, og de henger sammen. Det kinesiske selskapet Moonshot AI lanserte Kimi K3 — en modell med to komma åtte billioner parametere, som gjør den til verdens største open-weight AI-system. På benchmarktester måler den seg med de beste amerikanske frontmodellene. Markedene kalte det umiddelbart et nytt DeepSeek-øyeblikk: halvlederindeksen er nå ned over tjue prosent fra juni-toppen, Taiwan Semiconductor falt syv prosent til tross for at de rapporterte en sytti-syv-prosent økning i kvartalets driftsresultat, og Nvidia mistet kortvarig sin posisjon som verdens mest verdifulle selskap til Apple. Fullstendige modellvekter er planlagt offentliggjort den tjuesyvende juli.

Samme dag bekreftet Meta og Anthropic at de er i tidlige forhandlinger om en beregningskapasitetsavtale verdt ti milliarder dollar — der Meta tilbyr ledig GPU-kapasitet fra sin AI-infrastruktur over en toårsperiode.

Fra Norge: Regjeringen har forbudt generativ kunstig intelligens i barneskolen fra høsten av, for trinn én til sju. Statsminister Støre begrunnet det med bekymring for barns lese- og skriveutvikling. Dette skjer i samme uke som Stargate Norway bekreftet at de er i rute til å levere hundre tusen NVIDIA GPU-er til et datasenter i Narvik innen årets slutt, drevet av billig vannkraft.

Og i ettermiddag spiller Spania og Argentina VM-finalen i fotball.

Det var din søndagsoppdatering.

sources.title: US-Iran war: Trump orders new strikes as ceasefire collapses
Zelenskyy fires Ukraine's tech-savvy defense minister in government reshuffle
China's Moonshot AI releases Kimi K3, the largest open-source model ever, rivaling top U.S. systems
Tech stocks lead steep global selloff as investors lose faith in AI chip trade
Anthropic in talks to lease Meta computing power in $10 billion deal
Markets experience new DeepSeek shock after Moonshot AI releases Kimi K3
Zelenskyy's Dismissal Of Defense Minister Fedorov Sparks Protests
Norway Draws a Line on AI in Schools
Stock market next week: Outlook for July 20-24, 2026
World Cup final: Spain vs Argentina

https://www.cbsnews.com/live-updates/us-.title: US-Iran war: Trump orders new strikes as ceasefire collapses
https://www.cbsnews.com/live-updates/us-.excerpt: U.S. Central Command hit surveillance sites, military logistics infrastructure, underground weapons storage, and maritime capabilities during the seventh consecutive night of strikes against Iran. Trump ordered the new round of strikes to 'hold Iranian forces accountable.' Oil prices spiked back above $80 a barrel as the resumption of hostilities calls into question the path for inflation.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_.title: 2026 Iran war - Wikipedia
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_.excerpt: The 2026 Iran war began following the collapse of the June 17 Versailles ceasefire, in which the US and Iran agreed to end military fighting, allow ships to transit the Strait of Hormuz freely for at least 60 days, and lift sanctions on Iranian oil. On July 9, after Iran struck targets in the Strait of Hormuz, Trump declared the MOU over and US airstrikes resumed.

https://www.npr.org/2026/07/16/g-s1-1339.title: Zelenskyy fires Ukraine's tech-savvy defense minister in government reshuffle
https://www.npr.org/2026/07/16/g-s1-1339.excerpt: Ukraine's President Zelenskyy fired defense minister Mykhailo Fedorov, who pushed for innovation on the battlefield through the use of drones and turned the tables on Russia. The president said Fedorov had a systemic conflict with Commander-in-Chief Syrskyi and with the armed forces. Large protests erupted in several Ukrainian cities, with more than a thousand people taking part in a rally in Kyiv's central square.

https://venturebeat.com/technology/china.title: China's Moonshot AI releases Kimi K3, the largest open-source model ever, rivaling top U.S. systems
https://venturebeat.com/technology/china.excerpt: Moonshot AI unveiled Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion parameter open-weight model that on benchmark tests matched or outperformed several leading US models, trailing only Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and OpenAI's GPT-5.6. The full model weights are scheduled for public release on July 27, 2026, making it accessible to developers worldwide as an open-weight model.

https://fortune.com/2026/07/17/tech-stoc.title: Tech stocks lead steep global selloff as investors lose faith in AI chip trade
https://fortune.com/2026/07/17/tech-stoc.excerpt: Semiconductor stocks experienced a sharp downturn wiping out over a trillion dollars in market value, as Wall Street questions the sustainability of record AI capital spending. The Semiconductor Index shed more than 20% from its June peak. Taiwan Semiconductor fell 7% despite reporting a 77% jump in quarterly operating profit. Nvidia briefly ceded the world's most-valuable-company title to Apple.

https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/17/anthropi.title: Anthropic in talks to lease Meta computing power in $10 billion deal
https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/17/anthropi.excerpt: Meta Platforms is in early talks to lease computing power to Anthropic in a potential deal worth up to $10 billion over two years. Anthropic proposed the deal in June, and Meta is considering it, though discussions are in early stages and may not result in a deal. Such a deal would help Meta diversify beyond advertising by generating revenue from its AI infrastructure.

https://openai.com/index/introducing-sta.title: Introducing Stargate Norway
https://openai.com/index/introducing-sta.excerpt: Stargate Norway is planned to deliver 230MW of capacity with ambitions to expand by an additional 290MW, and the facility will target 100,000 NVIDIA GPUs by the end of 2026. Narvik's abundant hydropower, low-cost energy, cool climate, and mature industrial base make it an ideal location, representing one of the most ambitious AI infrastructure investments in Europe to date.

https://all-things-nordic.com/2026/06/22.title: Norway Draws a Line on AI in Schools
https://all-things-nordic.com/2026/06/22.excerpt: Norway's government announced that generative AI tools will not be allowed in elementary school starting in August 2026. Children in grades 1-7 will generally not be allowed to use tools such as ChatGPT in their education. Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Store said excessive reliance on AI risks undermining the development of essential skills, including reading, writing, and mathematics.

song_suggestion.title: 99 Luftballons
song_suggestion.why: A 1983 German pop song about a ceasefire that never comes — breezy melody, creeping dread underneath. The peace deal collapsed, the missiles are flying again, and yet the World Cup final is still on tonight.
